name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD organic/materials chemist with 6+ years in synthesis, purification, analytical characterization, and DOE-guided process development. Builds reproducible process windows and technical data packages connecting reaction/process variables to material quality, stability, robustness, and scale-up decisions.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided chemical process-development experiments for bio-based polymer systems, defining process windows, analytical readouts, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready scale-up specifications.
Translate process variables, FT-IR/thermal/spectroscopic data, and material-quality outcomes into technical recommendations and repeatable procedures.

Designed, synthesized, purified, and characterized 15+ organic electrochromic material variants, connecting molecular structure, purity, stability, and performance with NMR, GPC, MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA/DSC, and electrochemistry.
Diagnosed degradation and failure modes through hypothesis-driven experiments, analytical comparisons, and structured datasets supporting reaction/process iteration.
Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for molecular thin-film coatings, optimizing surface preparation and CVD conditions to improve reproducibility and optical/material performance.
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition under flow using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D monitoring to quantify growth kinetics and process controls.

Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration routines, SOPs, and safety protocols that improved instrument uptime by 30% and strengthened data integrity.

projects.title: DOE-Guided Process Development
projects.description: Built process windows, analytical readouts, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready specifications for bio-based polymer scale-up.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, FT-IR, thermal analysis, process variables

projects.title: Organic Synthesis and Analytical Characterization
projects.description: Synthesized and purified molecular variants, then connected structure, purity, stability, and performance through multi-technique data.
projects.tech: Organic synthesis, NMR, MS, GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, CV

projects.title: Process-Controlled Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Developed CVD/surface-preparation strategies and translated process conditions into reproducible material outputs.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, surface preparation, process optimization

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Process & Reaction Development: DOE, process characterization, route/reaction analysis, robustness/productivity studies, scale-up specifications, SOPs, QbD/cGMP/GDP readiness
skills.Analytical Characterization: NMR, LC-MS exposure, GC-MS, GPC/SEC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, QCM-D, cyclic voltammetry, chromatography-adjacent workflows
skills.Chemistry & Materials: Organic synthesis, purification, crystallization concepts, polymers, small-molecule materials, structure-property-process analysis
skills.Data & Documentation: SPC, Excel, Python/basic, JMP/basic, electronic/structured lab records, technical reports, publication-quality writing

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
